figure.title: PCS Multi-Flow Block Diagram
figure.purpose: Illustrates the PCS processing pipeline split across multiple concurrent flows (IFlow 0 and IFlow 1).

technical_summary: Data from the Reconciliation Sub Layer undergoes '66-bit block distribution' into multiple independent flows (IFlow 0 and IFlow 1).
Each IFlow independently performs Transcode, Scramble, Alignment Overwrite, Pre FEC distribution, FEC Encode, and Distribution and interleave.

On the receive side, lower-level functions (Alignment lock and deskew, Lane reorder) are shared, but then data is split back into IFlows for de-interleave, FEC Decode, Post-FEC interleave, Alignment Overwrite, Descramble, and Transcode.
Finally, '66-bit block collection' merges the flows back together.

components.name: 66-bit block distribution/collection
components.description: Splits/merges data across multiple processing pipelines.

components.name: IFlow 0 / IFlow 1
components.description: Parallel processing pipelines for PCS functions.

critical_facts: To support high bandwidth, the PCS logic is parallelized into multiple independent flows that operate concurrently on distributed 66-bit blocks.
